steps.title: Identify the triangle type
steps.content: Both legs measure 37, making this an isosceles right triangle with two 45° angles.

steps.title: Simplify to isolate the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Factor out the perfect square:
$$x = \sqrt{2738} = \sqrt{37^2 \cdot 2} = 37\sqrt{2}$$
Rewrite in the form requested. Since $37\sqrt{2} = \sqrt{37^2 \cdot 2} = \sqrt{2738}$ and $37^2 \cdot 2 = 1369 \cdot 2 = 2 \cdot 37^2$, the hypotenuse simplifies. For a 45-45-90 triangle, the ratio of leg to hypotenuse is $1:\sqrt{2}$, so hypotenuse $= 37\sqrt{2}$. This matches choice D — D is correct.
